weather_report:
Subiaco wakes to a rinsed-blue morning, the light already clean and insistent, shadows slimming on the pavement. The air feels honest and dry, and the day looks sharply drawn, almost high-definition. Expect the sun to rule through late morning, with the UV on the assertive side by midday, so the shade will be your quiet ally. Later, a thin lace of cloud may drift in, softening the glare without stealing the glow, and visibility should stay crisp from streetfront to treetop.

It reads as a pleasant, open day: uncomplicated, bright, and gently warming. Dress in light layers with breathable fabric, sunglasses, and a brimmed hat; a brush of sunscreen will go a long way. Good hours for a stroll to the bakery, a quick pedal to run an errand, airing the house, or tending a few pots on the balcony before the sun turns more forthright. After lunch, when the light eases a touch, linger with a book on a shaded bench or wander a quiet gallery, letting the afternoon drift at its own unhurried pace.
